frameworks.name: Bayesian observer
frameworks.one_line: optimal observer uses item-specific memory uncertainty in change-detection decisions

frameworks.name: point-estimate working memory
frameworks.one_line: alternative model in which only a stimulus point estimate is stored, without uncertainty

core_question: Do people maintain stimulus-specific uncertainty in working memory and use it implicitly when making subsequent decisions?
key_assumptions: decisions can be modeled as Bayesian inference over noisy memory representations
uncertainty information, if maintained, is available at decision time
manipulations: two conditions of an orientation change-detection task differing in whether uncertainty must be maintained vs. available only at decision
model comparison: Use Uncertainty vs. Ignore Uncertainty observer
key_findings: Use Uncertainty model fit better than Ignore Uncertainty model for all participants in both conditions
advantage holds in the condition that required maintaining uncertainty across the working memory delay
interpretations: people maintain item-specific memory uncertainty over a working memory delay
this uncertainty is used implicitly in a manner consistent with an optimal Bayesian observer
connecting_signals: model comparison Use vs Ignore Uncertainty
